Title: A closer look at the technologies, companies and products helping to secure the emerging Internet of Things.
Providers / Solutions
Description: These companies offer services tailored to large-scale IoT deployments, both to support industrial uses of IoT technology and as protection for cloud-connected consumer devices in the home.

Title: Books and Resources
Description: ​For a more comprehensive understanding of IoT security, check out our reading list of books, white papers and popular articles covering the topic.

Title: Best Practices / Guidelines
Description: A number of IoT industry groups have published recommendations for secure IoT development. Explore guidelines and best practices produced by the experts.

Title: Organizations and Standards
Description: These projects, foundations and alliances take a principled approach to promoting IoT security. Learn more about their work and how to get involved in the mission of protecting the Internet of Things.

Title: Additional resources
Description: Events

Infrastructure
Description: IPv6 - "IPv6, is an Internet Layer protocol for packet-switched internetworking and provides end-to-end datagram transmission across multiple IP networks.

Title: Data Protocols
Description:
MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)
"The MQTT protocol enables a publish/subscribe messaging model in an extremely lightweight way. It is useful for connections with remote locations where a small code footprint is required and/or network bandwidth is at a premium."

Title: Multi-layer Frameworks
Description: Alljoyn - An open source software framework that makes it easy for devices and apps to discover and communicate with each other.

Title: Vertical Specific
Description:
IEEE 1451:
The IEEE 1451, a family of Smart Transducer Interface Standards, describes a set of open, common, network-independent communication interfaces for connecting transducers (sensors or actuators) to microprocessors, instrumentation systems, and control/field networks.

Title: Architectures / Graphic
Description: Credit: Simon Ford - Director of IoT Platforms ARM

Title: Alliances and Organizations
Description:
Eclipse Paho Project
"The scope of the Paho project is to provide open source implementations of open and standard messaging protocols that support current and emerging requirements of M2M integration with Web and Enterprise middleware and applications. It will include client implementations for use on embedded platforms along with corresponding server support as determined by the community."

"The scope of the Paho project is to provide open source implementations of open and standard messaging protocols that support current and emerging requirements of M2M integration with Web and Enterprise middleware and applications. It will include client implementations for use on embedded platforms along with corresponding server support as determined by the community."

Title: A closer look at the technologies, companies and products helping to secure the emerging Internet of Things.
Providers / Solutions
Description: These companies offer services tailored to large-scale IoT deployments, both to support industrial uses of IoT technology and as protection for cloud-connected consumer devices in the home.

Title: Books and Resources
Description: ​For a more comprehensive understanding of IoT security, check out our reading list of books, white papers and popular articles covering the topic.

Title: Best Practices / Guidelines
Description: A number of IoT industry groups have published recommendations for secure IoT development. Explore guidelines and best practices produced by the experts.

Title: Organizations and Standards
Description: These projects, foundations and alliances take a principled approach to promoting IoT security. Learn more about their work and how to get involved in the mission of protecting the Internet of Things.
